url_verification_summary.competitor_section_strategy: session-side WebSearch at write time per §16.31 Wave 8 + Wave 9 5/5 dead-rate pattern + Wave 6/7 §16.43 confirmation. Stage 2 did not invent plausible firm URLs; RUN session sources fresh via Google Search at write time and applies §16.36 statutory-citation cross-check per page. Each pick's competitor section includes 6 suggested queries + competitor-drift-watchpoint flags (B19: stale 5-year-from-departure-date framing; stale no-UK-tax-once-non-resident framing; stale optional-split-year framing; stale non-residents-don't-file framing; stale domicile-determines-UK-tax framing now obsolete post-FA-2025. B20: stale DTA-exempts-UK-property framing; stale NRL-only-if-treaty-resolved framing; stale DTAs-automatic framing; stale all-UK-DTAs-OECD-Model-identical framing; stale OECD-article-numbers-interchangeable framing; stale non-dom+DTA framing now obsolete post-FA-2025).
url_verification_summary.stage2_webfetch_strategy: Stage 2 surfaced authority URLs from Stage 1 seed citations + cross-referenced §10 (DTAs general framing) + §16 in full (Wave 2 DTA extension locked 2026-05-22) + §16.1 (OECD article map) + §16.2 (Art 6 immovable property) + §16.3 (Art 13 capital gains + NRCGT override + UK-Luxembourg 2022 housekeeping lock signed 7 June 2022 / in force 22 November 2023 / effective UK IT/CGT 6 April 2024 / CT 1 April 2024) + §16.4 (Art 4 tie-breaker cascade — permanent home → centre of vital interests → habitual abode → nationality → MAP) + §16.5 (specific UK treaty notes including UK-US Art 1(4) saving clause + UK-France 2008 Art 24A + UK-India 1993 absence of Art 13(4) + UK-Spain 2013 Art 13(4)) + §16.6 (NRL-statutory-not-treaty — LOAD-BEARING for B20 Example 4 + cross-applied B19 Misframe 7) + §17 (Leaving the UK / expat full) + §17.1 (SRT cascade) + §17.2 (split-year Cases 1-3) + §17.3 (s.10A 5-year-or-less + 4-of-7-preceding gateway — LOAD-BEARING for B19 Example 4) + §17.4 (NRCGT TCGA 1992 s.1A + Schs 1A/1B/4AA) + §17.5 (NRL operational mechanics) + §17.6 (FA 2025 domicile reform FIG/TRF/LTR — Wave 5 lock; LOAD-BEARING for B19 Misframe 11 + B20 Misframe 10) + §22.X (IHT LTR 10-of-20 architecture) house position locks; per-page legislation.gov.uk + gov.uk + HMRC manual + OECD verification deferred to RUN session under §16.35 per-write discipline. Stage 1 author's per-citation verification on 2026-05-27 at TCGA 1992 s.10A (heading 'Temporary non-residents'; substituted by Finance Act 2019) + TIOPA 2010 s.18 (heading 'Entitlement to credit for foreign tax reduces UK tax by amount of the credit', Part 2 Chapter 2) holds clean across both picks. UK-Luxembourg 2022 dates verbatim from HP §16.3 carry into B20 Example sections + FAQ 9 + Misframe 4 grep. UK-US Art 1(4) saving clause per HP §16.5 carries into B20 Example 5 + FAQ 12 + Misframe 4 grep. HP §16.6 NRL-statutory-not-treaty carries verbatim into B19 Misframe 7 + B20 Example 4 + Misframe 2 + FAQ 4 grep (LOAD-BEARING for both picks).

discoveries_logged: D-1 STAGE2_INHERITED_VERIFICATION — Stage 1 author had already WebFetched + verified TCGA 1992 s.10A (heading 'Temporary non-residents'; substituted by Finance Act 2019 per legislation.gov.uk substitution note) + TIOPA 2010 s.18 (heading 'Entitlement to credit for foreign tax reduces UK tax by amount of the credit', Part 2 Chapter 2 under cross-heading 'Effect to be given to credit for foreign tax allowed against UK tax'; covers income tax, corporation tax, capital gains tax) at seed time on 2026-05-27. Stage 2 statutory-citation cross-check confirmed alignment with §10 + §16 in full + §16.1-§16.6 (Wave 2 DTA extension full lock) + §17 in full + §17.1-§17.6 + §22.X HP locks; no NEW drift catches at Stage 2 review.

D-2 STAGE2_CONTENT_DEPTH — the 2 Stage 1 seed briefs in this batch were unusually deep at the framing-differentiator + statutory-anchor + key-questions layers (continuing the pattern observed across M3-A-B1 D-2 + M3-A-B2 D-2 + M3-A-B3 D-2 + M3-A-B4 D-2 + M3-B-B1 D-2 + M3-B-B2 D-2 + M3-B-B3 D-2 + M3-C-B1 D-2 + M3-C-B2 D-2 + M3-C-B3 D-2 + M3-C-B4 D-2). B19 extension landed at +420 total lines (Stage 2 added 347 lines net); B20 extension landed at +391 total lines (Stage 2 added 306 lines net). Pattern across all MW3 batches confirms Stage 1 quality compounds Stage 2 efficiency.

D-3 STAGE2_LEAVING_UK_AND_DTA_INTRO_PAIR_DISCIPLINE — picks 1 (B19 do-i-have-to-pay-uk-tax — short-term-departure variant) and 2 (B20 dont-pay-twice-an-introduction-to-tax-treaties — general DTA orientation) form a deliberate 2-page non-resident-landlord pair within batch M3-B-B4. Cluster-level differentiation maintained at Stage 2: B19 = audience-specific four-overlapping-reasons opener for the 1-5-year overseas-stint landlord (SRT may keep you UK-resident + s.10A 5-year recapture + ITTOIA Part 3 unchanged + 60-day NRCGT regardless + IHT LTR throughout); B20 = general DTA orientation four-misconceptions opener (DTAs allocate not eliminate + NRL is statutory not treaty + foreign tax credit must be claimed + not all DTAs are identical). Cross-links bidirectional: B19 references B20 for the foreign-tax-credit + Art 4 tie-breaker framework that applies to its destination-state context; B20 references B19 for departure-context cross-link + B6 + B7 + B1 / B2 / B3 specific-DTA siblings.

D-4 STAGE2_HP_§16.6_DOUBLY_LOAD_BEARING — HP §16.6 (NRL-is-statutory-not-treaty) is doubly LOAD-BEARING in this batch: B19 Misframe 7 + FAQ 6 + Example 5 all rely on it (treaty residence does not displace NRL withholding for the short-term emigrant); B20 Example 4 + FAQ 4 + Misframe 2 all rely on it (the single most-misframed mechanic in cross-border property tax). Stage 2 ensures HP §16.6 quotation 'Treaty residence does not displace NRL; even a treaty-resolved non-UK resident must apply for gross-payment approval' carries verbatim across both picks. RUN session pairing of B19 + B20 should hold the line on NRL-statutory framing — under no circumstances paraphrase 'treaty-resolved non-UK resident' to 'tax treaty exempts UK rental' or analogous loose framings.

D-5 STAGE2_HP_§17.6_FA_2025_DOMICILE_REFORM_CROSS_PICK — HP §17.6 (FA 2025 FIG / TRF / LTR domicile reform — Wave 5 lock) carries cross-pick in this batch: B19 Misframe 11 surfaces 'domicile is the test' as LARGELY OBSOLETE post-6 April 2025; B20 Misframe 10 surfaces 'non-doms can use a DTA to avoid UK tax entirely' as LARGELY OBSOLETE on the same lock. Both Misframes mandatory in RUN session do-not-write GREP because pre-2025 commentary routinely conflates non-dom status with DTA mechanics; the FA 2025 reform decoupled the two (treaty residence + DTAs are SEPARATE from FIG/TRF/LTR). RUN session must surface this prominently in both pages.

D-6 STAGE2_HP_§17.3_S_10A_VERBATIM_LOAD_BEARING — HP §17.3 (TCGA 1992 s.10A 5-year-or-less + 4-of-7-preceding gateway) is LOAD-BEARING for B19 Example 1 (Singapore secondment Year 4 return analysis) + Example 4 (Swiss equity recapture with 3 counterfactuals at 4 / 5 / 6 years return) + FAQ 4 + Misframe 4 + Misframe 8 grep. Stage 1 verification against HMRC CG26540 on 2026-05-22 + Stage 2 cross-check confirms the 'COMPLETE tax years of non-UK residence' framing — NOT 5 calendar years from departure. Critical Misframe 8 grep: 'must be MORE THAN 5 complete tax years to escape' (NOT exactly 5; the 5-year window includes the 5th year). RUN session do-not-write GREP must catch any framing that says '5 years from departure date' or 'exactly 5 years escapes'.

D-7 STAGE2_BUG7_AUTONOMY_CONFIRMED — Bug #7 fix (work autonomously after acknowledgment line) worked cleanly across the 2 picks. No mid-batch pause; no Q-N raised; no blocker. Wall-clock matches the M3-A-B4 D-6 + M3-C-B4 D-6 patterns (~25-35 minutes for 2 substantive brief extensions on main branch with per-pick commit + final batch-done marker).

D-8 STAGE2_UK_LUXEMBOURG_2022_DATES_VERBATIM_CARRY — HP §16.3 Wave 5 housekeeping lock UK-Luxembourg dates (signed 7 June 2022; in force 22 November 2023; effective UK IT/CGT 6 April 2024; CT 1 April 2024) carry verbatim into B20 statutory anchor + worked-example FAQ 9 + Misframe 4 + Universal Rules voice + Anti-templating watchpoints. RUN session re-verifies at write per §16.35 because treaty operative dates are the kind of fact that can drift between Stage 2 and write (subsequent protocols or amendments).

D-9 STAGE2_HP_§16.1_ARTICLE_NUMBER_DISCIPLINE_BAKED — HP §16.1 (quote article number from SPECIFIC bilateral treaty, not OECD Model number) is operationalised in B20 throughout: UK-US Art 1(4) saving clause; UK-France 2008 Art 24A capital gains; UK-Luxembourg 2022; UK-India 1993 absence of Art 13(4); UK-Spain 2013 Art 13(4). RUN session must respect this discipline — when paraphrasing a specific bilateral treaty's provision, cite the bilateral-treaty article number, not the OECD Model article number. Misframe 11 grep mandatory.

D-10 STAGE2_FA_2025_FIG_TRF_LTR_CROSS_BATCH_CONSISTENCY — Stage 2 carries FA 2025 FIG / TRF / LTR domicile reform per HP §17.6 + §22.X across both picks. B19 Example 6 walks IHT LTR exposure during a 3-year stint via the 10-of-20 gateway test; B20 Misframe 10 surfaces non-dom + DTA framing as stale. Future MW3 sessions on the same cluster (B6 permanent-departure; B7 arriving-in-UK; the IHT-LTR-pillar; the domicile-reform-orientation pages) should maintain consistent FA 2025 framing — pre-2025 'non-dom' language is uniformly obsolete unless explicitly historical-context.
anti_templating_check: Two briefs read as a set. Boilerplate sections (Voice + style, 19-step workflow, Quality bar) are deliberately verbatim per §4.8 and §7. Substantive content (framing differentiators carried from Stage 1, worked examples, FAQ phrasing, anti-templating notes per pick) is distinct per slug. Each anti-templating note explicitly identifies sibling-collapse risk: B19 short-term-departure / temporary-non-residence variant (vs B6 permanent-emigration / >5-year clean-break sibling at the 5-year hinge; vs B7 arriving-in-UK inverse direction; vs B1/B2/B3 destination-specific DTA siblings; four-overlapping-reasons opener + Example 1 Singapore secondment + Example 4 s.10A recapture with 3 counterfactuals at 4 / 5 / 6 years return is the differentiator; Misframe 4 'measured by COMPLETE tax years' + Misframe 8 'must be MORE THAN 5 complete tax years' are load-bearing grep targets); B20 general DTA orientation / introduction page (vs country-specific siblings B1/B2/B3 which carry bilateral mechanics; this page introduces conceptual framework + four common misconceptions; four-misconceptions-table opener + Example 4 NRL-statutory-not-treaty + Example 5 UK-US saving clause + Example 6 foreign-tax-credit must be claimed is the differentiator; HP §16.6 verbatim 'Treaty residence does not displace NRL' is load-bearing for Misframe 2 grep + Example 4 + FAQ 4). No same-sentence drift detected across the set; each worked-example persona surname distinct per pick (B19: Penrose / Halloway / Carrington / Edenfield / Westerby / Tallaght; B20: Almeida / Romero / Hartmann / Anderson / Knowles). 11 distinct anonymised personas across 2 picks; no surname collisions within batch; no surname collisions against prior MW3 B-cluster batches (B-B1 / B-B2 / B-B3 personas all distinct). Pre-2025 'non-dom' framing is uniformly handled as obsolete (B19 Misframe 11 + B20 Misframe 10 — Stage 2 surfaces FA 2025 FIG / TRF / LTR per HP §17.6 + §22.X as the operative architecture). RUN session may freshen names if cross-batch persona register feels saturated.
worktree: C:/Users/user/Documents/Accounting (main repo, Stage 2 commits to main per dispatch prompt)
notes: Both picks extended on main branch. Each commit message uses 'MW3 Stage 2 M3-B-B4 pick N/2: <slug> extended' pattern for grep-driven manager triage. No Q&A raised; no flags raised; no NEW drift catches at Stage 2 cross-check (Stage 1 author's per-citation verification on 2026-05-27 holds clean across both picks). LOAD-BEARING CALL-OUTS for RUN session: (a) HP §16.6 (NRL-statutory-not-treaty) verbatim — engages B19 Example 5 + FAQ 6 + Misframe 7 + B20 Example 4 + FAQ 4 + Misframe 2 grep; the single most-misframed mechanic in cross-border property tax; quote verbatim 'Treaty residence does not displace NRL; even a treaty-resolved non-UK resident must apply for gross-payment approval'. (b) HP §17.3 (TCGA 1992 s.10A 5-year-or-less + 4-of-7-preceding gateway) verbatim — engages B19 Example 1 + Example 4 (with 3 counterfactuals) + FAQ 4 + Misframe 4 + Misframe 8 grep; must say 'COMPLETE tax years of non-UK residence' and 'MORE THAN 5 complete tax years to escape'; never 'from departure date' or 'exactly 5 years escapes'. (c) HP §17.6 (FA 2025 FIG / TRF / LTR Wave 5 domicile reform lock) — engages B19 Misframe 11 + B20 Misframe 10 grep; pre-2025 'non-dom' framing is LARGELY OBSOLETE post-6 April 2025; treaty residence + DTAs are SEPARATE from FIG/TRF/LTR architecture. (d) HP §22.X (IHT LTR 10-of-20 architecture) — engages B19 Example 6 + FAQ 9 + Misframe 10 grep; LTR test catches anyone with substantial UK residence history; a 3-year stint does not displace LTR status; worldwide IHT exposure persists throughout the stint. (e) TIOPA 2010 s.18 verbatim section heading 'Entitlement to credit for foreign tax reduces UK tax by amount of the credit' (Part 2 Chapter 2) — engages B19 FAQ 10 + Misframe 13 + B20 Example 6 + FAQ 10 + Misframe 3 grep; foreign tax credit must be CLAIMED, not automatic. (f) HP §16.3 UK-Luxembourg 2022 dates verbatim (signed 7 June 2022; in force 22 November 2023; effective UK IT/CGT 6 April 2024; CT 1 April 2024) — engages B20 FAQ 9 + Misframe 4 + Anti-templating watchpoints; verify at write per §16.35 because treaty operative dates can drift. (g) HP §16.5 UK-US Art 1(4) saving clause — engages B20 Example 5 + FAQ 12 + Misframe 4 grep; US citizen preserves US worldwide taxation regardless of UK residence; cannot 'treaty-out' of US taxation. (h) HP §16.1 article-number discipline — engages B20 throughout; quote article number from SPECIFIC bilateral treaty (UK-US Art 1(4); UK-France 2008 Art 24A; UK-Spain 2013 Art 13(4); UK-India 1993 absence of Art 13(4); UK-Luxembourg 2022 modernisation) not OECD Model number. (i) FA 2013 Sch 45 (SRT cascade Part 1 + split-year Cases 1-8 Part 3) verbatim — engages B19 Example 2 + Example 3 + FAQ 2 + FAQ 3 + Misframe 2 + Misframe 3 + Misframe 12 grep; split-year is STATUTORY not optional. (j) FA 1995 Sch 23 + SI 1995/2902 NRL — engages B19 Example 5 + FAQ 6 + Misframe 7 + B20 Example 4 + FAQ 4 + Misframe 2 grep; tenants / agents withhold 20% from gross rent regardless of treaty position unless NRL1 / NRL2 / NRL3 approval held. (k) Current NRCGT rates (18% / 24% from 30 October 2024) + AEA (£3,000 from 6 April 2024) — engages B19 Example 1 + FAQ 7 + B20 FAQ 7 grep; rate-by-reference verify at gov.uk per §16.35 (rates may shift in future Budgets). Cross-bucket cannibalisation note: clean cluster differentiation against B6 (permanent-departure sibling — distinguish at the 5-year hinge) + B7 (arrival inverse) + B1/B2/B3 (country-specific DTA siblings); both extension briefs differentiate explicitly + forward-link bidirectionally; no CANNIBAL flags. Potential F-numbered drift catch candidates for §16.43 mechanical back-patch sweep (all DEFERRED to RUN session verification at write): (i) existing site pages on tax-treaty-introduction or DTA-orientation may propagate the 'DTA exempts UK rental' myth — Stage 2 raises if confirmed at write; (ii) existing leaving-UK / non-resident-landlord pages may propagate the 'physical departure equals non-residence' myth or the '5-year-from-departure-date' s.10A myth — Stage 2 raises if confirmed at write; (iii) any existing site pages still using 'non-dom' framing without FA 2025 FIG/TRF/LTR housekeeping update may be stale — Stage 2 raises if confirmed at write. All three candidates documented for RUN session §16.36 cross-check pass + manager triage at §16.43 back-patch sweep decision.
